description.en: To use this feature, you must be holding thieves' tools or artisan's tools. Then, as an Action, you touch a Tiny nonmagical object and imbue it with one magical property of your choice. Maximum - {{value}}.
The ability to infuse certain magic into ordinary objects and turn them into magical ones. Infusions - {{infusions}}, attuned items - {{items}}.

title.en: The Right Tool for the Job

description.en: Using thieves' tools or artisan's tools, you can magically create one set of artisan's tools in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. This requires 1 hour of uninterrupted work, which can be done during a short or long rest. Although created through magic, the tools themselves are nonmagical and disappear when you use this feature again.
Your proficiency bonus is doubled on all ability checks made using a tool you are proficient with.
When you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an ability check or saving throw, you can use your reaction to add {{value}} to the roll.
If you create a magic item with a rarity of «common» or «uncommon», it will take you a quarter of the usual time to create it and half as much gold.

You can touch one simple or martial weapon or item that can be used as a spellcasting focus and store in it one 1st or 2nd level spell from the artificer spell list that requires 1 action to cast. A creature can use its action to release the spell effect from the item, attack bonus - {{attack}}, DC - {{dc}}, uses - {{uses}}.
You ignore all class, race, spell, and level requirements when attuning or using magic items.

You gain a +1 bonus to all saving throws for each item you are currently attuned to. If you are reduced to 0 hit points but are not killed, you can use your reaction to destroy one of your infusions, reducing your hit points to 1 instead of 0.
Roll on the Experimental Elixir table to determine the effect that occurs when someone drinks the elixir.
Whenever you cast a spell using alchemist's tools as a focus, you gain a {{value}} bonus to one of your spell rolls. That roll must restore hit points or deal acid, fire, necrotic, or poison damage.
